author: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice
book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.

metadata.author: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice

title: Canon IX.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: If any presbyters have been advanced without examination, or if upon examination they have made confession of crime, and men acting in violation of the canon have laid hands upon them, notwithstanding their confession, such the canon does not admit; for the Catholic Church requires that [only] which is blameless.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: Whoever are ordained without examination, shall be deposed if it be found out afterwards that they had been guilty.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: The crimes in question are those which were a bar to the priesthood—such as blasphemy, bigamy, heresy, idolatry, magic, etc.—as the Arabic paraphrase of Joseph explains.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: It is clear that these faults are punishable in the bishop no less than in the priest, and that consequently our canon refers to the bishops as well as to the πρεσβύτεροι in the more restricted sense.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: These words of the Greek text, “In the case in which any one might be induced, in opposition to the canon, to ordain such persons,” allude to the ninth canon of the Synod of Neocæsarea.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: It was necessary to pass such ordinances; for even in the fifth century, as the twenty-second letter to Pope Innocent the First testifies, some held that as baptism effaces all former sins, so it takes away all the impedimenta ordinationis which are the results of those sins.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: Some say that as baptism makes the baptized person a new man, so ordination takes away the sins committed before ordination, which opinion does not seem to agree with the canons.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: This canon occurs twice in the Corpus Juris Canonici.

verses.book: The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
The Canons of the 318 Holy Fathers Assembled in the City of Nice, in Bithynia.
verses.text: Dist. xxiv. c. vij., and Dist. lxxxj., c. iv.
